note: Paraphrased summary of the four core functions, scoped to agent deployment; not verbatim text.

passages.title: GOVERN: accountability, roles, and ownership
passages.text: The GOVERN function calls for clear roles, responsibilities, and lines of accountability for AI systems. Each system should have an accountable owner so that risk decisions and remediation have a responsible party. An agent with no accountable owner cannot be governed.

passages.title: MAP: establish context, purpose, and intended use
passages.text: The MAP function establishes the context and intended purpose of an AI system. A specific, declared purpose lets reviewers bound the system's scope of authority; a vague or missing purpose is itself a risk because authority cannot be scoped.

passages.title: MEASURE: assess risks before and during deployment
passages.text: The MEASURE function assesses, analyzes, and tracks AI risks using repeatable evaluations. Pre-deployment validation against known failure modes (for example, unsafe tool boundaries or sensitive-data handling) provides evidence for an onboarding decision.

passages.title: MANAGE: prioritize and apply controls; record decisions
passages.text: The MANAGE function allocates resources to treat the highest risks and documents the controls applied. Boundaries on tools, data, providers, and budget, plus human approval for high-impact actions, are management controls that should be recorded as evidence.
